output_summary: aligned schemas for datasets: kaggle_small, uber_eats_small, yelp_small; generated 2 schema correspondence groups.
profiled datasets: kaggle_small, uber_eats_small, yelp_small.
Reused the blocking configuration from state.

Blocking uses `semantic_similarity` for kaggle_small_uber_eats_small, uber_eats_small_yelp_small, blocking on name_norm, city, state; `exact_match_multi` for kaggle_small_yelp_small, blocking on phone_e164, postal_code.
Performance-wise, pair completeness is 1.00 for all pairs and candidate counts range from 179 to 199972.
The config also records dataset id columns.

matcher mode `ml` with config source reused from state; evaluated pairs: _update_attempts, id_columns, matching_strategies; selected scores: matching_strategies (threshold=0.75, f1=0.8947368421052632, f1=0.983050847457627, threshold=0.72, f1=0.9302325581395349); produced 3 pair strategy entries.
This run was an initial draft of the pipeline adaption.
The pipeline was updated to use embedding-based and standard blocking strategies for three dataset pairs.
Feature extractors were configured with string and numeric comparators tailored to each dataset pair.
Ground truth datasets were loaded for training and testing the matchers.
GridSearchCV was employed to select the best machine learning models for each dataset pair.
MLBasedMatcher was used for entity matching with the trained classifiers.
Maximum Bipartite Matching was applied to the matching results.
The fusion strategy was defined with 'longest_string' fuser for nine attributes and 'union' fuser for the 'categories' attribute.
The pipeline now supports embedding-based blocking alongside standard blocking methods.
Matching behavior incorporates multiple ML models selected via cross-validation for improved accuracy.
Fusion behavior predominantly uses the 'longest_string' strategy for most attributes, with 'union' used specifically for the 'categories' attribute.
Singleton entities are excluded from the fusion process.
The pipeline includes helper functions for preprocessing string attributes such as lowercasing and stripping.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.

Added extensive entity matching pipeline in pipeline.py including loading datasets, blocking with EmbeddingBlocker and StandardBlocker, defining comparators and feature extractors for three dataset pairs, loading ground truth, extracting features, hyperparameter tuning with GridSearchCV over RandomForest, LogisticRegression, GradientBoosting, and SVM, training best models, matching entities, applying MaximumBipartiteMatching clustering, saving correspondences.
This run was an initial draft of the evaluation adaption.
The dominant evaluation strategy used was a column-specific evaluation function registration within a DataFusionStrategy named 'restaurant_fusion_strategy'.
Tokenized match functions were applied to name, name_norm, street, house_number, city, state, postal_code, and country fields.
Numeric tolerance match functions were applied to latitude, longitude, rating, and rating_count fields.
Set equality match functions were applied to categories and other detected list-like columns after normalization.
Year-only match function was applied to the open_date field if present.
Added list-like column detection and normalization to improve evaluation accuracy on list fields.
Implemented ID alignment diagnostics to compare direct _id coverage and mapped eval_id coverage against gold standard IDs.
Enhanced evaluation output with debug logging and JSON formatted debug files.
Persisted evaluation results to a JSON file for downstream use.
The evaluation adaption fits the current run by providing a comprehensive and tailored evaluation strategy for the restaurant fusion dataset, leveraging appropriate matching functions per field type.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 81.23%; evaluation attempt counter is 1.
The fusion pipeline is missing some gold entities entirely due to exclusion of singletons in fusion (include_singletons=False).
There are many attribute-level false negatives caused by overly strict evaluation rules, especially exact_match used for address_line1, which punishes minor formatting differences.
The rating attribute has very low accuracy (40%) due to no proper rating fusion and too strict numeric tolerance in evaluation.
Identity and provenance fields (_id, source, map_url) have very low accuracy due to schema and semantic mismatches rather than true fusion errors.
A single gold record kaggle380k-001820 repeatedly appears with fused_id: NaN and reason: "missing_fused_value" across many attributes, indicating it is missing from fused output.
High accuracies (above 90%) for core identifiers like phone_e164, house_number, postal_code, latitude/longitude, name, city/state/country, categories, and website.
Moderate accuracies for address_line1 (80%), street (85%), and name_norm (80%).
Very poor accuracies for rating (40%), _id (27.5%), source (27.5%), and map_url (35%).
Debug events show many near matches differing only by casing, abbreviations, or minor formatting (e.g., 'McDowell Rd' vs 'Mcdowell Rd', 'St' vs 'Street').
This run was an evaluation refinement of the existing pipeline.
The pipeline currently excludes singletons by setting include_singletons to False.
Fusion uses the longest_string fuser for 9 attributes and union fuser for categories.
No changes to fusers or thresholds were made in this run.
The fusion strategy remains naive, primarily using longest_string without domain-aware logic or source preference.
Matching behavior is stable and produces clean 1-to-1 matches with high confidence, so no shift in matching strategy occurred.
Blocking uses embedding-based and exact match blockers with pre-optimized configurations.
Matching uses MLBasedMatcher with classifiers selected by GridSearchCV.
Evaluation revealed key issues: missing fused entities due to singleton exclusion, strict evaluation rules punishing minor formatting differences, and lack of rating fusion.
No normalization or domain-specific fusion logic is applied to addresses, phones, or ratings.
Excluding singletons causes false negatives where gold entities appear only once and are missing from fused output.
Using longest_string fuser is simple but leads to errors when sources have conflicting or differently formatted values.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.

Updated pipeline.py to ensure explicit id columns named 'id' in datasets; added os.makedirs("output/data_fusion", exist_ok=True) for fusion output directory; refined evaluation.py imports to include load_xml and load_parquet; modified _detect_gold_prefix to split on underscore instead of dash; adjusted build_eval_view to assign eval_id without string conversion; improved ID coverage diagnostics to handle missing columns safely.
This run was an evaluation refinement of the existing fusion pipeline.

The dominant evaluation strategy used was a combination of tokenized_match for string fields, numeric_tolerance_match for numeric fields, set_equality_match for list-like fields, and year_only_match for date-like fields.

Core identifiers and location fields such as phone_e164, house_number, postal_code, latitude, longitude, name, city, state, country, categories, website, and rating_count showed very good accuracy (mostly above 90%).
Address_line1, street, and name_norm fields had moderate accuracy around 80%, mainly due to cosmetic differences and strict evaluation rules.
Rating accuracy was very poor at 40%, indicating issues with numeric tolerance and fusion logic for ratings.
Identity and provenance fields like _id, source, and map_url had very low accuracy (27.5% to 35%) due to schema and semantic mismatches rather than true content errors.
The evaluation strategy was updated to register tokenized_match for additional string fields including address_line1, address_line2, phone_raw, phone_e164, source, website, and map_url.
Numeric tolerance matching was applied to rating and rating_count fields to better handle small numeric differences.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 72.09%; previous logged accuracy was 81.23%; evaluation attempt counter is 2.
The main problem in this run is attribute-level fusion and evaluation errors caused primarily by schema, typing, and preprocessing issues.
Phone fields are completely broken with zero accuracy due to numeric storage and string comparison mismatches.
Map URLs and ratings have weak accuracy due to lack of normalization and naive evaluation.
ID and source tracking fields have very low accuracy because of misalignment between fused cluster IDs and gold IDs.
Street-level attributes have moderate accuracy issues caused by formatting and abbreviation differences.
Fusion logic is under-specified for many evaluated attributes, with missing fusers leading to nulls or inconsistent values.
Phone fields phone_e164 and phone_raw have 0.0 accuracy with all 40 records wrong.
Map URL accuracy is 0.35 and rating accuracy is 0.4, indicating poor fusion or evaluation.
_id and source accuracies are 0.275, showing poor alignment with gold IDs.
Street accuracy is 0.85 and address_line1 accuracy is 0.875, showing some formatting mismatches.
Debug logs show phone values are numerically equal but differ as strings due to float conversion (e.g., '16024284444.0' vs '16024284444').
This run was an evaluation refinement of the existing pipeline.
No changes to attribute fusers or thresholds were made in this run.
The pipeline continues to use 'longest_string' as the dominant fuser for nine attributes and 'union' for the categories attribute.
Singleton retention remains disabled (include_singletons=False).
There was no shift in fusion or matching behavior; the pipeline maintains embedding-based blocking and string/numeric comparison support.
The fusion strategy remains minimal and under-specified for many evaluated attributes, focusing on a simplistic longest_string approach.
The evaluation revealed critical schema and preprocessing issues, especially with phone fields stored as floats causing evaluation mismatches.
Debug analysis showed that blocking and matching quality are already excellent, so no changes were made to those components.
The evaluation highlighted the need for improved fusion and evaluation alignment, particularly for phone number handling, street normalization, and URL evaluation.
Refinement focused on diagnosing and understanding dominant error types rather than changing the pipeline immediately.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.

pipeline.py updated comments for explicit id columns and saving correspondences filenames; evaluation.py changed import to only load_csv from PyDI.io; replaced restaurant fusion strategy name with "ml_fusion_strategy"; replaced multiple explicit register_eval calls with loops over string, numeric, date-like, and list-like columns for evaluation function registration; added printing of compact evaluation functions summary line.
This run was an evaluation refinement.
The dominant evaluation strategy used was tokenized_match for textual attributes and numeric_tolerance_match for numeric attributes.
Phone fields (phone_e164, phone_raw) showed complete failure due to schema and evaluation mismatches.
Street-level address fields (street, address_line1) had moderate accuracy with mismatches caused by formatting and abbreviation differences.
Map URL and rating fields had weak accuracy due to naive URL comparison and evaluation strictness.
ID and source fields had low accuracy due to cluster ID and gold ID misalignment and lack of fusion for source.
Other fields such as name, lat/lon, city/state/country/postal_code, website, categories, rating_count, and address_line2 had high accuracy (≥ 0.92).
Updated evaluation strategy comments to better match fusion pipeline naming.
No changes to evaluation functions were explicitly made in this run, but the evaluation setup was clarified and aligned with fusion logic.
List-like columns were detected and normalized before evaluation.
ID alignment diagnostics were included to assess coverage of fused IDs against gold IDs.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 71.96%; previous logged accuracy was 72.09%; evaluation attempt counter is 3.

Added extensive new code in pipeline.py implementing data loading from parquet and CSV, blocking with EmbeddingBlocker and StandardBlocker for three dataset pairs, defined string and numeric comparators with feature extractors, loaded ground truth training sets, extracted features, performed GridSearchCV hyperparameter tuning for RandomForest, LogisticRegression, GradientBoosting, and SVM models, selected best models, matched entities with MLBasedMatcher.
